shower.thought: The worst part about being right is that you get to watch people prove you right in slow motion.
You can't remember most of your life, which means you've already forgotten who you were most of the time.
We teach children not to lie while simultaneously teaching them that Santa, the Tooth Fairy, and their parents' happiness are all real.
If you had to explain why you love someone to a stranger, you'd probably realize you're not sure anymore.
The people who've hurt you most are usually the ones who had the best access to your vulnerabilities.
You only appreciate a good parking spot when you've had a really bad one.
Nostalgia is just grief wearing a better outfit.
We're all just trying to be the person someone once made us feel like we could be.
The most honest thing about you is probably something you've never said out loud to anyone.
Time moves differently depending on whether you're waiting for something or running from it.
You know you're old when the music from your childhood starts playing in grocery stores and elevators.
The saddest relationships aren't the ones that end—they're the ones that just become roommates.
Most people don't actually want advice; they want validation that they were right to feel the way they did.
We spend decades building a version of ourselves for other people, then wonder why we feel empty.
The scariest moment isn't when you realize you've changed—it's when you realize you haven't.
If everyone you know left your life tomorrow, you'd discover who you actually are instead of who you pretend to be.
Your phone knows more about your real priorities than you do.
The moments that change your life rarely feel significant when they're happening.
We call it "finding ourselves" when really we're just getting tired of pretending.
Forgiveness isn't about letting them off the hook—it's about finally putting down the rope you've been holding onto.

motivational.thought: Your limitation today is the only qualification you need for tomorrow. — Unknown
The difference between who you are and who you want to be is what you do. — Bill Phillips
Comfort is the enemy of growth, and growth is the price of peace. — Unknown
You are not behind. You are not lost. You are exactly where you need to be to become who you're meant to be. — Unknown
Every expert was once a beginner who refused to stay that way. — Unknown
The life you want doesn't require a different person. It requires a different decision. — Unknown
Doubt whispers that you can't. Action proves it wrong. — Unknown
You don't rise to the level of your goals—you fall to the level of your systems. — James Clear
What would you attempt if you knew you couldn't fail? Now do it anyway. — Unknown
The moment you're ready to quit is usually the moment right before the breakthrough. — Unknown
Your future self is watching you right now through memories you haven't made yet. — Unknown
Small disciplines repeated with consistency are far more powerful than motivation. — Unknown
You can't heal what you won't acknowledge. You can't grow what you won't face. — Unknown
Success is not about being the best. It's about being better than you were yesterday. — Unknown
The cost of discipline is always less than the cost of regret. — Unknown
Nobody is coming to save you. And that's the most liberating news you'll ever hear. — Unknown
Character is what you do when nobody's watching. Excellence is doing it anyway. — Unknown
Your scars are not your weakness. They're proof you survived. — Unknown
The only way out is through. The only way through is to start. — Unknown
Progress is the only perfection that matters. — Unknown

darkjoke.thought: My doctor told me I have six months to live. I said I can't pay the bill. He gave me another six months.
I'm on a seafood diet. I see food and I remember I have no money.
My therapist asked if I ever feel like a burden to my family. I said, "Only on weekends when they visit."
I told my boss I was thinking about quitting. He said, "Please don't make any sudden movements."
My life is like a horror movie, except the killer is just really, really slow.
I asked my friend how his divorce was going. He said, "I'm winning custody of my regrets."
My dating profile says I'm looking for someone to complete me. My bank account says I'm not picky.
I tried to be a minimalist, but that just meant having fewer things to hide my problems with.
My dentist said I should floss more. I said, "I should do a lot of things," and we both sat in silence.
I'm not saying my job is soul-crushing, but my LinkedIn profile picture is just getting progressively darker.
My mom asked if I was eating enough vegetables. I said, "I'm eating enough regret."
I told my ex I wanted closure. She said, "The door's right there."
My therapist asked about my childhood trauma. I said, "Which therapist are you billing this to?"
I have a very strict morning routine: I wake up and immediately regret it.
My friend said life is about the journey, not the destination. I said, "Tell that to my credit card debt."
I asked the barista for something to take the edge off. He made my coffee black.
My doctor asked if I drink responsibly. I said, "I drink exactly as much as someone with my future should."
I told my landlord I was having trouble with the rent. He said, "Join the club." Turns out we both hate living here.
My spouse asked what I was thinking about. I said, "How much therapy this moment will cost later."
I tried to join a gym to work on myself. Turns out I needed more fundamental repairs first.
